skill.name: agent-tool-builder
skill.description: Guides AI agents through tool schema design, tool descriptions, validation, error handling, and MCP-oriented tool planning.
skill.summary: Design reliable AI agent tools with clear schemas, descriptions, validation, and recoverable errors.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review found these are false positives in documentation text. SKILL.md contains guidance about AI tool design and related-skill names, with no executable code, network behavior, secret access, or prompt-injection instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Backticked Skill Names
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at line 53. The line lists related skill names in Markdown inline code and contains no executable command, shell invocation, or user-controlled input path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is a Markdown related-skills list, not code. No surrounding content creates an execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns across documentation lines. Review found prose about AI agents, schemas, descriptions, validation, and anti-patterns, with no cryptographic API use or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is documentation only and does not call cryptographic libraries. The flagged lines do not define, recommend, or execute weak algorithms.

content.value_statement: AI agents fail when tool schemas and descriptions are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design tools with clear inputs, useful errors, and predictable behavior.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create clear JSON Schema definitions for AI tools.
Shows how tool descriptions guide model behavior during function calling.
Covers validation patterns for inputs and outputs.
Describes recoverable error messages that help agents continue work.
Identifies common tool design anti-patterns, including vague descriptions and too many tools.
Frames tool design around MCP-compatible agent workflows.
limitations: Does not provide runnable tool server code.
Does not include examples for a specific programming language framework.
Requires the user to adapt guidance to their agent platform.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Agent Tool
use_cases.description: Define the tool purpose, required inputs, output shape, and error behavior before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Function Calling Reliability
use_cases.description: Rewrite vague tool descriptions and schemas so models choose the right tool with fewer retries.

use_cases.title: Review MCP Tool Interfaces
use_cases.description: Check whether a tool interface is clear, bounded, and recoverable for agent workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Describe a Simple Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a simple AI tool for [task]. Define its purpose, inputs, output, and one useful error response.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Tool Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this tool description for ambiguity: [description]. Rewrite it so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code know when to use it.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Tool Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this tool schema for missing constraints, unclear field names, and validation gaps: [schema summary]. Provide specific improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Agent Tool Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a small tool set for [agent workflow]. Separate tool responsibilities, define recoverable errors, and explain why each tool is necessary.

output_examples.input: Design a tool that lets an agent search project issues.
output_examples.output: A proposed tool specification with a clear search purpose, required query input, optional filters, result limits, and errors for empty queries or unavailable issue systems.

output_examples.input: Review a vague tool description for a deploy tool.
output_examples.output: A revised description that states when to deploy, what inputs are required, what confirmations are needed, and how failures should be reported.

output_examples.input: Create error handling guidance for a file lookup tool.
output_examples.output: A list of recoverable error cases, including missing path, permission failure, multiple matches, and suggestions the agent can act on.

best_practices: Give each tool one clear responsibility and a specific usage boundary.
Use explicit field names, validation constraints, and examples in schemas.
Return errors with enough context for the agent to recover or ask a better question.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on vague tool descriptions that hide when the tool should be used.
Do not create large overlapping tool sets that force unnecessary model choice.
Do not return silent failures or generic errors that block recovery.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design AI agent tools, including schemas, descriptions, validation rules, and error responses.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance that developers can apply when building tools.

faq.question: Can it help with MCP tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes MCP-oriented guidance for clear tool interfaces and agent workflows.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other function-calling agents.

faq.question: What is the main benefit?
faq.answer: It helps agents use tools more reliably by improving descriptions, schemas, and recoverable errors.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or access external systems?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance and does not contain executable tool code.
